creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
(In reply to Matthias Andree from comment #1)
> Mikhail, I do not see such an argument
Here is the quote:

>> This is subtle, but if you look at the .valgrind files that were provided in
>> the openexr.zip linked in the first comment of the bug, you'll see that
>> exr2aces is the program that was used to reproduce these errors.

>> That's important and subtle because exr2aces isn't built by default, and is
>> therefore not shipped by many Linux distributions either.

> update to OpenEXR and ilmbase 2.2.1 committed as r455244.

Well, that's nice too.
